rationale: GitHub Actions added a 'Prevent self-review' option for environment deployments. Previously, users who triggered a workflow could also approve their own deployments if they were required reviewers. The new workflow adds a configuration step (s5) to enable self-review prevention and enforces a waiting step (s9) where the user who triggered the workflow cannot approve it - a different reviewer must approve the deployment. This ensures deployments are reviewed by at least two different people, improving security for critical environments.
